events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 10 March: Displacement, supply shocks and civilian toll rise
events.description: The conflict in the Middle East continues, with strikes and counter-strikes across the region and the humanitarian toll mounting. UN agencies warn that rising civilian casualties, mass displacement – particularly in Lebanon – and damage to critical infrastructure are deepening the crisis, while disruptions to shipping and energy routes risk driving up global prices and worsening food insecurity. Stay with us for updates from across the United Nations system. UN News app users can follow the coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 10 March: Displacement, supply shocks and civilian toll rise

events.title: Asia scrambles to confront energy crisis unleashed by Iran war – with no end in sight
events.description: From fuel caps to four-day work weeks, the Middle East conflict has left the world’s top crude oil importing region desperate to shore up supplies Donald Trump has scrambled in recent days to reassure the world that the economic impact of his war on Iran can be contained. Sure, one of the most important waterways in global trade has, in effect, been shut for almost two weeks – but it might reopen before long. In the meantime, US oil-related sanctions on “some countries” will be lifted. And besides, the entire conflict could be over soon . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Asia scrambles to confront energy crisis unleashed by Iran war – with no end in sight

events.title: Death toll from Ethiopia floods and landslides rises to more than 100
events.description: Forecasters say storms are becoming more intense in the region, partly due to global warming.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Death toll from Ethiopia floods and landslides rises to more than 100

events.title: Quit fossil fuels to stem deadly floods in Brazil’s coffee heartland, say scientists
events.description: Global heating linked to rising risk of extreme rain that causes devastating landslides and rising coffee prices The record floods that have brought death and destruction to the heartland of Brazil’s coffee industry are expected to intensify if people continue to burn fossil fuels, analysis has shown. Dozens of residents in the state of Minas Gerais have been buried alive in landslides or swept away as roads turned into rivers over the past month. Thousands more have been forced to evacuate their homes, while the wider, longer-term effects are likely to include higher prices for coffee across the world. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Quit fossil fuels to stem deadly floods in Brazil’s coffee heartland, say scientists

events.title: FBI opens terrorism investigation after deadly shooting at Virginia university
events.description: The FBI opened a terrorism investigation after a gunman opened fire Thursday at Old Dominion University in Norfolk, Virginia, killing one person and wounding two US Army personnel, officials said. The suspect, previously convicted of aiding terrorists, was subdued by ROTC students after entering a classroom and asking if it was ROTC class, officials.
events.ticker_label: 💥 FBI opens terrorism investigation after deadly shooting at Virginia university

events.title: Israel strikes central Beirut after threatening to expand Lebanon operations
events.description: Israel renewed its strikes on Beirut on Thursday, as it threatened to expand operations and seize territory in Lebanon if Hezbollah did not stop its attacks. The Israeli military issued a call to evacuate ahead of the Beirut attack, after having also widened its evacuation warning for residents in southern Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel strikes central Beirut after threatening to expand Lebanon operations

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 12 March: Shipping attacks, rising oil prices and widening humanitarian crisis
events.description: War continues across the Middle East, with impacts growing far beyond the region. Oil prices are hovering around $100 a barrel as attacks on shipping and energy infrastructure disrupt key supply routes. Strikes and counterstrikes between Israel, Hezbollah and Iran continue, while ships have reportedly been hit in the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman, sending shocks through the global economy. Meanwhile, UN agencies are expanding humanitarian relief efforts as displacement rises and civilian needs grow. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 12 March: Shipping attacks, rising oil prices and widening humanitarian crisis

events.title: Israel hits back after coordinated Iran-Hezbollah missile, drone strikes, urges Beirut to rein in terrorists
events.description: Hezbollah fired 200 missiles and drones into Israel in what Israeli media called an integrated attack with Iran, prompting fierce Israeli Defense Forces strikes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel hits back after coordinated Iran-Hezbollah missile, drone strikes, urges Beirut to rein in terrorists

events.title: At least 17 killed after drone strikes school in Sudan
events.description: Strike in Shukeiri killed schoolgirls, teachers and healthcare workers in latest incident in three-year war At least 17 people, most of them schoolgirls, were killed on Wednesday when an explosive-laden drone blamed on Sudan’ s paramilitary Rapid Support Forces struck a secondary school and a health care centre. At least 10 people were wounded in the strike in the village of Shukeiri in the White Nile province, according to Dr Musa al-Majeri, director of Douiem hospital, the nearest major medical facility to the village. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 At least 17 killed after drone strikes school in Sudan

events.title: World News in Brief: Deadly drone attacks in Sudan, violence against women in the spotlight, more clashes in DR Congo
events.description: UN human rights chief Volker Türk said on Thursday he was appalled by the devastating impact on civilians of increasing drone attacks in Sudan, amid reports that more than 200 civilians have been killed by drones since 4 March alone, in the Kordofan region and White Nile state.

events.title: Iran regime cited as Trump admin set to designate Sudan’s Muslim Brotherhood a terror group
events.description: Trump administration designates Sudanese Muslim Brotherhood as terrorist organization amid Sudan's civil war that has seen thousands of deaths and millions of displacements.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran regime cited as Trump admin set to designate Sudan’s Muslim Brotherhood a terror group

events.title: U.S. investigates strike on Iranian school as the war sparks a global oil crisis
events.description: Trump, who promised to lower gas prices, is tapping the Strategic Petroleum Reserve as war drives prices up. And, the U.S. investigates the strike on an Iranian school that killed at least 165 people.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. investigates strike on Iranian school as the war sparks a global oil crisis

events.title: Russia jails 15 men for life over concert hall terror attack
events.description: Fifteen men received life sentences for the Crocus City Hall attack that killed 150, including children, and wounded hundreds inside a burning building. It was Russia’s deadliest such incident in two decades.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Russia jails 15 men for life over concert hall terror attack

events.title: With focus on Iran, Israeli settlers wage West Bank violence
events.description: As Israel and the United States continue their war on Iran, settlers are pushing deeper into Palestinian land in the West Bank. At least five Palestinians have been killed in a series of recent attacks by settlers.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 With focus on Iran, Israeli settlers wage West Bank violence

events.title: UK government axes flagship global health project
events.description: Programme which supports schemes in six African countries was previously hailed as vital protection for Britain against future pandemics A flagship health project in Africa, which UK ministers said would play a vital role in protecting Britain from future pandemic threats, is being axed due to aid cuts, the Guardian can reveal. The Global Health Workforce Programme (GHWP) which supported development and training for healthcare staff in six African countries, will close at the end of the month, the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) said. Continue reading...

events.title: How the Iran conflict is becoming a global crisis
events.description: 👉Listen to The World with Richard Engel and Yalda Hakim on your podcast app👈

events.title: World News in Brief: Myanmar airstrikes worsen crisis, South Sudan fighting threatens Akobo, Afghan women shut out of justice
events.description: Conflict continues to fuel suffering and drive humanitarian needs in Myanmar, the UN relief coordination office, OCHA, said in a bulletin on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Myanmar airstrikes worsen crisis, South Sudan fighting threatens Akobo, Afghan women shut out of justice

events.title: Iran war could spark the next global food crisis
events.description: The Iran conflict is driving up energy and fertilizer costs, raising alarms over a return of food inflation. Farmers are bracing for resource shortages that could drag down harvests.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war could spark the next global food crisis

events.title: There's a dangerous new addition to warfare in Sudan - and it's a death trap for civilians
events.description: A drone is circling in the skies above Zaki Ramadan when he takes my call. As a humanitarian worker in Sudan's southeastern Blue Nile state, he is helplessly watching aid dwindle and civilians be killed by the meteoric rise in drone strikes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 There's a dangerous new addition to warfare in Sudan - and it's a death trap for civilians

events.title: Russian court convicts 19 people over deadly 2024 Moscow concert hall attack
events.description: A Moscow court has convicted 19 people of involvement in the 2024 shootings that killed 149 and injured more than 600 at a concert hall in Moscow
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Russian court convicts 19 people over deadly 2024 Moscow concert hall attack

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 11 March: Civilian toll rises, regional disruption spreads as Security Council meets
events.description: As the conflict in the Middle East continues to ripple across the region, the UN Security Council met today to discuss the escalating situation, with a morning briefing focused on Lebanon and a second session later on the wider crisis – adopting a Bahrain-led resolution, while a Russian-led text failed to secure enough votes. Across the UN system, agencies are warning of mounting humanitarian needs as displacement rises, services come under strain and regional instability disrupts mobility, trade and aid operations. Stay with us as we bring you the latest developments, reactions and updates from UN Headquarters and across the region. UN News app users can follow the coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 11 March: Civilian toll rises, regional disruption spreads as Security Council meets

events.title: Suspect killed after attack on Detroit-area synagogue
events.description: A rifle‑wielding man rammed his car into one of the largest Reform synagogues in the United States. The suspect was dead and a security guard was injured in the ramming.

events.title: Iran's supreme leader issues first statement since his appointment
events.description: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei has issued his first statement since his appointment as the war with the United States and Israel rages. The statement was read on state television Thursday by a news anchor. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's supreme leader issues first statement since his appointment

events.title: Security Council adopts Gulf-backed resolution condemning Iran attacks; Russia bid fails
events.description: As war continues in the Middle East, the UN Security Council on Wednesday adopted a Bahrain-led resolution condemning Iranian attacks on several regional states, while a separate draft resolution introduced by Russia on the wider crisis failed to pass.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council adopts Gulf-backed resolution condemning Iran attacks; Russia bid fails

events.title: Iran war live: Trump says war going ‘well’ as Gulf under wave of attacks
events.description: Israeli strikes kill dozens more in Lebanon, including 5 children among nine people killed in attack on village of Arki.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war live: Trump says war going ‘well’ as Gulf under wave of attacks

events.title: Three merchant ships struck as tensions rise in Hormuz strait amid Iran war
events.description: Crew of Thai-registered bulk carrier forced to flee fire, as US says it has destroyed 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels Middle East crisis live – latest updates Three merchant ships have been struck in and around the strait of Hormuz, including a Thai registered bulk carrier that caught fire after leaving a port in the UAE, forcing crew members to evacuate for their safety. The Mayuree Naree was struck on Wednesday by “two projectiles of unknown origin”, its owners said, as it sailed about 11 nautical miles north of Oman, marking the end of a four-day lull of attacks in the strategic waterway. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Three merchant ships struck as tensions rise in Hormuz strait amid Iran war

events.title: Meta disables more than 150,000 accounts in crackdown on south-east Asian scam networks
events.description: Company also launches tools to spot scammers as Thai police arrest 21 people Meta disabled more than 150,000 accounts and Thai police arrested 21 people in a sweeping international crackdown on south-east Asian criminal scam centers that targeted people around the world, the social media company said on Wednesday. The operation was led by Thailand ’s Royal Thai police anti-cyber scam center, alongside the FBI and the US justice department’s scam center strike force, with Meta investigators acting on intelligence shared in real time by law enforcement. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Meta disables more than 150,000 accounts in crackdown on south-east Asian scam networks

events.title: From Sudan to Lebanon: ‘Wherever we go, war follows us’
events.description: In Beirut, refugees from conflict zones like Sudan and Ethiopia have once again been caught up in war — the very thing they hoped to leave behind in their home countries. FRANCE 24's reporter spoke to families sheltering at a Jesuit church in the Lebanese capital, who have lost everything for a second time after fleeing Israeli and US air strikes on Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 From Sudan to Lebanon: ‘Wherever we go, war follows us’

events.title: Iran's new supreme leader injured in strikes which killed his father
events.description: Iran’s new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei was wounded in the US-Israeli airstrikes which killed his father.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's new supreme leader injured in strikes which killed his father

events.title: ‘Perfect storm’: Lebanon crisis deepens as civilians bear the brunt
events.description: Lebanon is facing a “perfect storm of unpredictable challenges” as conflict, mass displacement and dwindling humanitarian resources converge, the UN’s Resident and Humanitarian Coordinator in Lebanon, Imran Riza, has warned.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ ‘Perfect storm’: Lebanon crisis deepens as civilians bear the brunt

events.title: An ‘alarming’ two-thirds of children say cyberbullying has increased
events.description: Around two-thirds of children worldwide report an increase in cyberbullying, with one in two say they don’t know how to get the right support, according to a recent poll carried out by the UN’s top official who works to end violence against children.
events.ticker_label: 💥 An ‘alarming’ two-thirds of children say cyberbullying has increased

events.title: US diplomatic facility in Iraq struck by drone
events.description: U.S. diplomatic facility in Baghdad hit by suspected retaliatory drone strike amid State Department warnings for Americans to leave Middle East countries due to Iran conflict escalation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US diplomatic facility in Iraq struck by drone

events.title: Two people die after donating plasma at Canadian clinics under federal investigation
events.description: Company that runs the sites says it has ‘no reason to believe there is a correlation between the donors’ passing and plasma donation’ Two people have died in Canada after donating plasma at a chain of clinics that has been under scrutiny by federal inspectors for failing to keep accurate records, screen donors or maintain its machines. While experts say the deaths are exceedingly rare, critics say Canada’s embrace of private companies to handle blood products reflects a “slow collapse of a system that has been the envy of the world”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Two people die after donating plasma at Canadian clinics under federal investigation

events.title: Man fatally shot after ramming pickup truck into Michigan synagogue
events.description: Police shot and killed a man on Thursday after he drove a vehicle into a synagogue in West Bloomfield, a suburb of Detroit, Michigan, where a large-scale law enforcement operation is now under way. The city's county sheriff confirmed the fatality and reported that a security guard had also been injured.

events.title: 30% of Europe’s cocaine routed through West Africa region
events.description: In tonight's edition, a new report warns that trafficking routes from Latin America to Europe are increasingly passing through the West Africa region. Also, the new US ambassador to South Africa backtracks on his criticism of the 'Kill the Boer' chant. And over a month after Cyclone Gezani devastated eastern Madagascar, the storm’s survivors are facing a new crisis as the price of construction materials has surged by 50 percent.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ 30% of Europe’s cocaine routed through West Africa region

events.title: New challenges bring increased risks in combating child sexual abuse, exploitation
events.description: Despite some progress made globally, children around the world still face serious and evolving risks of being sold, sexually exploited and abused, the UN independent human rights expert on the sale, sexual exploitation and sexual abuse of children, Mama Fatima Singhateh, warned on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New challenges bring increased risks in combating child sexual abuse, exploitation

events.title: Canada’s Carney under pressure to act after synagogues shot at in latest antisemitic incidents
events.description: Gunfire attacks on Toronto synagogues highlight Canada's rising antisemitism crisis, as hate incidents against the country's Jewish community continues.

events.title: Rubio designates Afghanistan as 'state sponsor of wrongful detention': 'Despicable tactics'
events.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio designated Afghanistan as a state sponsor of wrongful detention, accusing the Taliban of using terrorist tactics against Americans.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Rubio designates Afghanistan as 'state sponsor of wrongful detention': 'Despicable tactics'

events.title: French aid worker among three killed in dronestrike in east DRC, M23 rebels say
events.description: Rebel group blames government for attack on residential area of M23-controlled city of Goma Three people including a French UN aid worker have been killed in a drone attack in Goma, a spokesperson for the M23 rebel group has said. The attack took place at about 4am on Wednesday in the upmarket residential neighbourhood of Himbi in the city, which has been under M23 occupation since January 2025. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 French aid worker among three killed in dronestrike in east DRC, M23 rebels say

events.title: The war in Iran, its effect on the Middle East and the growing global oil crisis
events.description: The latest on the Iran war and the growing oil crisis across the Middle East from Dubai, Iraq, Lebanon and Israel.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The war in Iran, its effect on the Middle East and the growing global oil crisis

events.title: Threats to the Strait of Hormuz raises concerns about global oil prices
events.description: The threat of Iran laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz -- the narrow waterway through which about 20% of the world's oil supply travels -- is raising fresh fears about global oil prices.

events.title: Netanyahu says Israeli strikes killed Iranian nuclear scientists
events.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said several Iranian nuclear scientists were killed in Israeli strikes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Netanyahu says Israeli strikes killed Iranian nuclear scientists

events.title: Iran war: Experts warns bombing oil infrastructure in Iran to leave lasting environmental damage
events.description: The fallout from fighting in the Middle East, from burning oil refineries to sunken ships, threatens lasting repercussions for the health of the region's people, water and food supplies, and ecosystems.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Experts warns bombing oil infrastructure in Iran to leave lasting environmental damage

events.title: Humanitarian crisis ‘worsening’ in Afghanistan as war erupts in the Middle East
events.description: Crises in the region on both Afghanistan’s longest borders are undermining the country’s stability, a senior UN official warned the Security Council on Monday as concerns over Middle East crisis grow amid clashes with Pakistan and a worsening humanitarian crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Humanitarian crisis ‘worsening’ in Afghanistan as war erupts in the Middle East

events.title: NYC boosts patrols amid ‘heightened threat environment,' after gunman rams truck into Michigan synagogue
events.description: New York City ramps up security as an attack was reported at a Michigan synagogue, leaving a security guard unconscious. Officials condemned 'horrifying' antisemitic violence.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 NYC boosts patrols amid ‘heightened threat environment,' after gunman rams truck into Michigan synagogue

events.title: US military announces rescue effort after fuelling aircraft crashes in Iraq
events.description: No deaths have been reported so far, but the military said the crash involved two aircraft, one of which landed safely.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ US military announces rescue effort after fuelling aircraft crashes in Iraq

events.title: Iran to allow India-flagged tankers to pass through Strait of Hormuz (reports)
events.description: Iran will reportedly allow Indian-flagged tankers to transit the Strait of Hormuz, a conduit for 40% of the South Asian nation's crude imports, an Indian government source told Reuters on Thursday, but an Iranian source outside the country denied any such ​deal was reached. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Giacome Luciani, Professor at the Paris School of International Affairs.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Iran to allow India-flagged tankers to pass through Strait of Hormuz (reports)

events.title: US Democrats call for 'swift investigation' into strike on Iranian school
events.description: A group of 46 mostly Democratic senators on Wednesday sent a letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth seeking answers on the bombing of a school in Iran. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US Democrats call for 'swift investigation' into strike on Iranian school

events.title: Moscow court sentences 15 men to life in prison for jihadist attack on concert hall
events.description: A Russian court sentenced four perpetrators and eleven accomplices to life imprisonment on Thursday for the March 2024 attack on Moscow’s Crocus City Hall, which killed 150 people, making it the country’s worst jihadist attack in more than 20 years.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Moscow court sentences 15 men to life in prison for jihadist attack on concert hall

events.title: World News in Brief: Türk’s South Sudan ceasefire call, Ukraine strikes, Gaza food alert, Afghan returnees
events.description: UN human rights chief Volker Türk on Tuesday condemned accounts of “gruesome killings” of civilians in South Sudan, the destruction and poisoning of key water sources and fresh waves of mass displacement.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Türk’s South Sudan ceasefire call, Ukraine strikes, Gaza food alert, Afghan returnees

events.title: Unexplained loud bangs perplex neighbors near homes of alleged NYC terror plot suspects
events.description: Mysterious explosions plagued Bucks County for months near homes of men now accused in an alleged ISIS-inspired NYC terror plot. Police found no evidence, Fox News Digital confirmed.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Unexplained loud bangs perplex neighbors near homes of alleged NYC terror plot suspects

events.title: Fuel shortages bring queues, protests around the world
events.description: Rising fuel prices have caused long queues and protests around the world as the US-Israeli war on Iran rages.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fuel shortages bring queues, protests around the world

events.title: At least 50 people killed and 125 others reported missing after Ethiopia landslides
events.description: An official says at least 50 people have died and 125 others are missing after landslides hit three districts in southern Ethiopia following a week of heavy rains
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ At least 50 people killed and 125 others reported missing after Ethiopia landslides

events.title: Iraq says at least one killed in Basra port attack as country halts operations at oil ports
events.description: Iraq says at least one killed in Basra port attack as country halts operations at oil ports

events.title: Ukraine: Kyiv's anti-drone tech in demand in Gulf and NATO
events.description: The war in the Middle East is dominating the news but Ukraine is still under fire from Russia. Kyiv is now offering insight into shooting down Iranian drones in exchange for continued support from its allies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Kyiv's anti-drone tech in demand in Gulf and NATO

events.title: Middle East war: Dubai targeted by fresh Iranian drone strikes
events.description: #Dubai was among the targets of fresh Iranian drone strikes early on March 12, alongside Kuwait's airport. This comes after #Iran denounced a #UN Security Council resolution that demanded strikes on the #Gulf countries stop. Eleven civilians have died since the start of Iranian attacks in the region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: Dubai targeted by fresh Iranian drone strikes

events.title: What we know about the deadly Iran school strike
events.description: Evidence is growing that outdated US intelligence was "likely" to blame for the attack on a school in southern Iran which killed more than 170 people, most of them children.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What we know about the deadly Iran school strike

events.title: ‘No one should be a hibakusha’: Young Japanese activist’s mission to share atomic bomb survivor stories
events.description: A Japanese peace activist is hoping to promote global nuclear disarmament by keeping alive the stories of the atomic bomb survivors through the creation of a mobile museum.
events.ticker_label: 💥 ‘No one should be a hibakusha’: Young Japanese activist’s mission to share atomic bomb survivor stories

events.title: Suspect dead after ramming truck into Michigan synagogue and opening fire, FBI and ATF responding
events.description: Authorities responded to an active shooting in West Bloomfield, Michigan, after a suspect allegedly rammed a vehicle into the Temple Israel synagogue and was killed by security.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Suspect dead after ramming truck into Michigan synagogue and opening fire, FBI and ATF responding

events.title: Netanyahu says Israel ‘stronger than ever’ in first speech since Iran war
events.description: Prime minister says US-Israeli war prevented Iran from moving its nuclear and ballistic projects underground.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Netanyahu says Israel ‘stronger than ever’ in first speech since Iran war

events.title: Iraqi official urges Australia to take back alleged IS fighters during meeting with ambassador
events.description: Iraq’s national security adviser Qassim al-Araji says he told Australian ambassador that countries should repatriate prisoners Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A senior Iraqi government official has implored Australia to repatriate a group of suspected Islamic State fighters, raising the issue with Canberra’s top diplomat in Baghdad just weeks after the detainees were transferred out of Syria. In a post on X, Iraq’s national security adviser, Qassim al-Araji, said he met with ambassador Glenn Miles last week, and told him that foreign detainees should be returned to their home countries. This is despite such a task being made more difficult by growing instability in the region, caused by the war in Iran. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iraqi official urges Australia to take back alleged IS fighters during meeting with ambassador

events.title: With Middle East in flames, Texan bunker maker sees business boom
events.description: Since the war in the Middle East began nearly two weeks ago, the phone at Ron Hubbard's bomb shelter company in Texas hasn't stopped ringing. Foreign and US clients are rushing to buy his bunkers, seeking refuge in case of air raids, nuclear fallout or apocalypse.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 With Middle East in flames, Texan bunker maker sees business boom

events.title: With Middle East ‘pushed to breaking point’, UN chief urges ceasefire, return to diplomacy
events.description: The UN chief on Thursday urged all combatants to cease hostilities in the Middle East, uphold international law, protect civilians and make an immediate return to the negotiating table.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 With Middle East ‘pushed to breaking point’, UN chief urges ceasefire, return to diplomacy

events.title: Nicaragua: Rights experts uncover State corruption fuelling repression, spying on exiles
events.description: Nicaragua’s Government is financing the repression of its opponents through illegal misuse of public funds and targeting exiles through a transnational surveillance and intelligence network, the UN Group of Independent Human Rights Experts on Nicaragua warned in its latest report on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Nicaragua: Rights experts uncover State corruption fuelling repression, spying on exiles

events.title: Emirati minister tells BBC Iran must end strikes on Gulf
events.description: The UAE says more than 1,800 drones and missiles have been projected at the country since the war began.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Emirati minister tells BBC Iran must end strikes on Gulf

events.title: Senegal approves tougher anti-gay law as rights groups raise concerns
events.description: "Violence and fear" will spike and "equality and non-discrimination" will be trampled on, warn critics.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Senegal approves tougher anti-gay law as rights groups raise concerns

events.title: Serbia imports more arms than other Western Balkan states
events.description: Data from the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute indicates that, while military spending across the Western Balkans has increased, Serbia is the No. 1 importer of major weapons systems in the region.

events.title: UN commission opens with focus on justice for women and girls
events.description: Access to justice for women and girls dominated the agenda on Monday as the Commission on the Status of Women opened its 70th session at UN Headquarters in New York. Government officials, civil society representatives and UN leaders called for renewed efforts to dismantle the discrimination and legal obstacles that continue to limit women’s and girls’ rights worldwide.

events.title: Suspected Old Dominion University gunman ID'd as former National Guard soldier convicted of supporting ISIS
events.description: A suspected gunman in Virginia was killed Thursday after opening fire inside Constant Hall at Old Dominion University, injuring two individuals.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Suspected Old Dominion University gunman ID'd as former National Guard soldier convicted of supporting ISIS

events.title: Germany to adjust heating law to allow fossil fuels
events.description: Fossil fuel prices are rising in response to global conflicts and military actions. Still, Germany is revising its policy for heating private homes to focus less on renewables. Climate activists are up in arms.

events.title: Who gives the kill order? AI and the war in Iran
events.description: Warfare's always evolving, sometimes slowly: Napoleon’s armies traveled only as those of Julius Caesar. Sometimes, faster than commanders can process: see the futile carnage of World War I. The pace of change on the battlefield today? Fast, very fast.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Who gives the kill order? AI and the war in Iran

events.title: ‘Toxic rain’ warning from oil depot strikes amid ongoing Middle East war
events.description: Toxic “black rain” linked to strikes on oil depots, mass displacement and continuing disruption to aid supply chains are upending lives across the Middle East and beyond after 10 days of war in the region, UN humanitarians said on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Toxic rain’ warning from oil depot strikes amid ongoing Middle East war

events.title: Michigan synagogue ramming an ‘act of targeted violence’: FBI
events.description: The FBI says it is treating a vehicle ramming at a Michigan synagogue as an attack on the Jewish community.

events.title: ‘Invasive’ AI-led mass surveillance in Africa violating freedoms, warn experts
events.description: Countries across the continent have spent more than $2bn on Chinese tracking technology that is not ‘necessary or proportionate’, new report finds The rapid expansion of AI-powered mass-surveillance systems across Africa is violating citizens’ right to privacy and having a chilling effect on society, according to experts on human rights and emerging technologies. At least $2bn (£1.5bn) has been spent by 11 African governments on Chinese-built surveillance technology that recognises faces and monitors movements, according to a new report by the Institute of Development Studies, which warns that national security is being used to justify implementing these systems with little regulation. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Invasive’ AI-led mass surveillance in Africa violating freedoms, warn experts

events.title: Capital gains tax discount ‘overwhelmingly’ benefits investors in Australia’s richest electorates, analysis shows
events.description: Chief executive of Acoss, who undertook the study, says ‘it’s clear this tax break funnels billions into the wealthiest parts of our country’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Investors who live in the wealthy electorate of Wentworth in Sydney’s eastern suburbs claimed about $1.8bn from the 50% capital gains tax discount, according to new research. It reveals how a handful of rich enclaves in Australia’s two biggest cities account for a fifth of the annual benefit from the tax break. The Australian Council of Social Services is lobbying for a halving of the CGT discount and has used analysis of Australian Taxation Office data from 2022-23 to highlight how the benefits “flow overwhelmingly to a small number of high-income, inner-city electorates in the eastern states”. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran says its new leader made his 1st address, vowing to keep Strait of Hormuz closed
events.description: Iran's state media issued what it said was a statement by Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, vowing to keep the Strait of Hormuz closed and keep up attacks on U.S. bases in the region.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: A young girl is knocked over at Tokyo crossing – what’s behind Japan’s ‘bumping’ trend?
events.description: Viral video of girl being shoved by fellow pedestrian has reignited debate over butsukari – with experts blaming stress and gender dynamics It starts out as a heartwarming clip. A young girl, clearly delighted to be in Tokyo, beams as she makes a peace sign to the camera. Seconds later, she is shoved to the ground from behind by a woman wearing a surgical mask. The assailant doesn’t skip a beat, striding out of shot of the clip filmed by the girl’s mother. This was no accidental clash of shoulders in a crowded place, but one of the most visible examples of a spate of butsukari otoko – “bumping man” – shoving incidents in Japan that experts attribute to a combination of gender dynamics and the stresses of modern life. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A young girl is knocked over at Tokyo crossing – what’s behind Japan’s ‘bumping’ trend?

events.title: How oil price hikes threaten Germany's economy
events.description: How high will the Iran war drive up the prices of oil, gasoline and diesel? There is great concern that the price shock could dash hopes for economic recovery in Germany.

events.title: Australia news live: Bowen announces new measures to address fuel supply issues; Qantas to pay $105m to settle Covid flight credit dispute
events.description: Follow updates live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Joyce says surge in support for One Nation reflects will of the people, not political jostling Barnaby Joyce spoke to RN Breakfast this morning about One Nation’s targets in the next federal election. We want to win seats wherever they are. We have no real target against National seats or Liberal seats, but we want to give people the option to vote for us in Labor seats, in National seats, and Liberal seats, and in teal seats. If people choose to vote for One Nation, then you must respect that choice. You must understand. You do not own their vote. You earn their vote. Continue reading...

events.title: Argentina grants asylum to Brasília rioter in move that may sway Brazil vote
events.description: Decision to shield pro-Bolsonaro truck driver sentenced for 8 January 2023 attack could inflame Brazil election politics Argentina has granted asylum to a Brazilian fugitive convicted for his role in 2023 pro-Bolsonaro riots – a decision that analysts say could reverberate in Brazil ’s upcoming presidential election. A week after Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, Brazil’s president, took office, hundreds of people ransacked Brazil’s congress building, presidential palace and supreme court on 8 January 2023, in an attempt to overturn former president Jair Bolsonaro’s electoral defeat. Investigators later concluded the attacks were the culmination of a broader plot aimed at staging a coup . Continue reading...

events.title: Netanyahu says new Iran leader 'can't show his face in public'

events.title: Oil prices top $100 as market braces for long conflict
events.description: Brent crude oil prices have once again topped $100 per barrel, as energy markets brace for the Iran conflict to drag on. US President Donald Trump says he's less concerned about high oil prices than about stopping Iran, while his energy secretary has confirmed the military cannot yet escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. Also in the show - Indian households and businesses are facing high prices and shortages of cooking fuel, linked to the Gulf disruptions.

events.title: ‘You are the foundation-shakers’: UN chief answers tough questions at townhall with women’s civil society groups
events.description: The UN chief on Tuesday applauded civil society groups for “shaking the foundations of privilege” in a male-dominated world, addressing a range of questions during a townhall meeting on the margins of the 70th session of the Commission on the Status of Women (CSW70) at UN Headquarters.

events.title: The Pussycat Dolls confirm reunion: 'We're celebrating where we're at as women'

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Eswatini says it received more ‘third country’ deportees as part of deal with Trump administration
events.description: Two deportees sent to Eswatini were from Somalia, one was from Sudan and one was from Tanzania The government of Eswatini announced on Thursday it received four more “third country” deportees from the United States, as part of the Trump administration ’s multimillion-dollar deal with the small African nation. Now, a total of 19 deportees from the US have been sent to Eswatini when they hail from other countries, amid the Trump administration’s continued anti-immigrant crackdown and changes to immigration policy. Continue reading...

events.title: China’s rubber-stamp parliament set to approve ‘ethnic unity’ law
events.description: New legislation will require schools to use Mandarin by default, taking priority over minority ethnic languages such as Tibetan, Uyghur and Mongolian China’s National People’s Congress (NPC), the state legislature, will vote on Thursday on a suite of new laws agreed at this year’s annual two sessions gathering , including a piece of legislation that will diminish the role of minority ethnic languages in the education system. NPC delegates are expected to approve a new ethnic unity law, along with a new environmental code and the 15th five-year plan, the economic planning document for 2026-2030. Delegates have spent the last week debating Beijing’s proposed bills, which they are all but certain to approve. The NPC, which is often described as a rubber-stamp parliament, has never rejected an item on its agenda. Continue reading...

events.title: The race for Paris: Will more armed police and more cameras make the city safer?
events.description: With France preparing to vote in the first round of municipal elections on Sunday, Paris has become a battleground between left- and right-wing forces fighting to shape the capital’s future. And as Parisians increasingly place public safety at the top of their concerns, candidates from across the spectrum have pledged to pour more funds into the city’s municipal police force and surveillance system – measures that have shown little evidence of actually tackling crime.

events.title: NSW minister ‘not going anywhere’ as calls mount for independent inquiry into children living with convicted killer
events.description: Child welfare advocates demand accountability after revelations two children in care of state lived with triple killer Regina Arthurell Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A New South Wales minister says she is “not going anywhere” amid mounting calls for an independent review into why the state government allowed a convicted triple killer to live in a home with two foster children. The NSW minister for families and communities, Kate Washington, apologised on 2GB radio on Wednesday morning after confirming that Regina Arthurell had been removed from a home on Monday where she lived alongside children aged 12 and 14. The removal came after the radio revealed the situation earlier that day. Continue reading...

events.title: US tech giants on Iran's list of potential targets in Gulf countries
events.description: Iran's Tasnim news agency has published a list of potential targets for Iran on Telegram, including the offices of tech giants such as Amazon, Google, Microsoft and Nvidia in Gulf countries and Israel. Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports from Doha for FRANCE 24.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: José Antonio Kast, the Pinochet fan about to swerve Chile to the far right
events.description: The new president won office by promising to clean up crime, but his background is red rag to a bull for many Just south of Santiago, the tiny rural town of Paine is a quiet grid of painted adobe facades, shaded squares and shuttered shop fronts as the summer holidays draw to a close. But the white-knuckle fear of crime that propelled its most famous son, José Antonio Kast, to a resounding victory in December’s presidential election is as present in sleepy Paine as it is the length of Chile. Continue reading...

events.title: French vote tests polarised electorate with right hoping to win control of Paris
events.description: The highest-profile contest is for the mayorship of Paris - which has been under left-wing control for 25 years.

events.title: Israeli military drops charges against soldiers accused of abusing Palestinian detainee
events.description: The Israeli military's top lawyer cites "exceptional circumstances" for dropping the case involving a Palestinian man held at Sde Teiman military prison.

events.title: Parents accused of hiding daughter's HIV infection and neglect
events.description: The child did not see a doctor until she was six - and had not had any vaccinations, Italian media say.

events.title: Epstein used modelling agent to recruit girls, Brazilian women tell BBC
events.description: Modelling agent used businesses to recruit girls and arrange US visas to visit Jeffrey Epstein, Brazilian women tell BBC.

events.title: Nasa spacecraft weighing 1,300lb re-enters Earth's atmosphere
events.description: Much of the Van Allen Probe was expected to burn up in the atmosphere, though Nasa said there was a "low" risk of people being struck by surviving components.

events.title: Jo Malone sued for using her own name in collaboration with Zara
events.description: The perfumier sold the rights to her name in 1999 but has previously said she regretted the move.

events.title: Iran envoy says Tehran will keep Strait of Hormuz open
events.description: Iran’s UN envoy Amir Saeid Iravani said Tehran will not close the Strait of Hormuz and remains committed to freedom.

events.title: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah sweeps to power in Nepal landslide election victory
events.description: Rastriya Swatantra Party, founded just four years ago, set to dominate new parliament with near two-thirds majority.

events.title: Taiwan cheering row overshadows China quarterfinal at Asian Cup

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: El Salvador’s mass arrest policy may have led to crimes against humanity, study shows
events.description: Experts documented murder, torture and disappearances under Nayib Bukele’s policy targeting gangs The draconian mass incarceration policy of El Salvador’s president, Nayib Bukele, may have led to crimes against humanity, according to a new study by legal experts. By locking up 1.4% of the population without due process, Bukele turned El Salvador from one of Latin America’s most violent countries into one of its least violent – but at the cost of human rights and the rule of law. Continue reading...

events.title: Khameni's killing raises old questions about U.S. assassinating foreign leaders
events.description: The killing of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei raises questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be in the business of assassinating foreign leaders.

events.title: Artemis II launch 'on track' for as soon as April, says NASA
events.description: Riddled by technical delays, NASA's Artemis II mission would mark humanity's first trip to the moon in half a century.

events.title: Could Germany adopt AI giant Anthropic?
events.description: After Donald Trump ordered the US government to cancel all contracts with Anthropic, a German politician has said Germany should offer to bring the AI firm to Europe. Is that viable?

events.title: Germany: 7-year-old flashes €5,000 cash at Osnabrück school
events.description: The youngster found a cash-filled envelope at home that his parents had withdrawn to buy a car, but not before he took it to class for an extra-curricular "if I were a rich man" lesson with his friends.

events.title: China passes controversial 'ethnic unity' law
events.description: The new law means Mandarin will become the official languages for purposes such as education and public affairs. According to human rights groups, the move could contribute to the marginaliation of minorities.

events.title: Elon Musk's Tesla experiment: Unchecked ambition costs lives
events.description: A documentary explores how Tesla drivers serve as unpaid beta testers of the company's self-driving cars while Elon Musk focuses on his long-term goal: colonizing Mars.

events.title: WAFCON 2026: A 'combination of factors' behind postponement
events.description: African football's governing body CAF says it made the decision to postpone it's top women's tournament to ensure "the best possible conditions." It's not the first time the tournament has been moved at short notice.

events.title: Cape leopard's return sparks hope
events.description: In South Africa's Western Cape mountains, the return of a top predator is giving conservationists hope after 170 years. But with fewer than a thousand Cape leopards left in the wild, the species remains dangerously vulnerable.

events.title: Midget subs and kamikaze drone boats - how Iran can block the Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The fear of mines laid by submarines, kamikaze drone boats and GPS jamming have effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Record number of political parties register for Haiti's first election in a decade
events.description: A record 280 political parties and counting have registered to participate in Haiti’s first general election in a decade

events.title: Venezuela's Machado calls Trump a 'fundamental ally' despite US support for Delcy Rodríguez
events.description: María Corina Machado says the Trump administration remains a key ally for Venezuela’s democratic transition, even as Washington backs interim President Delcy Rodríguez

events.title: Mexico's president promises 'Plan B' after congressional defeat of electoral reforms
events.description: Mexico’s President Claudia Sheinbaum says she has a “Plan B” after Congress blocked her electoral reform, her first major defeat in office

events.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine awaits White House sign-off on US drone production deal
events.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he is awaiting White House approval for a major drone production agreement proposed by Kyiv last year

events.title: Celebrity chef resigns from iconic Danish restaurant Noma after abuse allegations
events.description: René Redzepi, founder and celebrity chef at the iconic Danish restaurant Noma, has stepped down from his post following allegations of abuse and assault at the Copenhagen landmark

events.title: China adopts an ethnic unity law that critics say will cement assimilation
events.description: China has adopted a sweeping ethnic unity law that critics say tightens assimilation and weakens minority rights

events.title: Maryland teen charged as adult for alleged stabbing in high school bathroom
events.description: Teen allegedly stabs classmate at Great Mills High School in Maryland, charged as adult with assault. Victim, 19, released from hospital and recovering.

events.title: FBI’s iconic Most Wanted list enters new era with unprecedented addition
events.description: FBI makes history adding first alleged cybercriminal to Ten Most Wanted list. Anibal Aguirre accused of ATM jackpotting scheme funding Venezuelan gang.

events.title: Judge says no new trial for Laken Riley killer Jose Ibarra
events.description: Breaking: Georgia judge denies Jose Ibarra's motion for new trial in Laken Riley murder case. Venezuelan migrant's life sentence without parole remains intact.
